vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the observed physical reality. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not clearly identifiable as distinct specimens; the black pot contains only a single, struggling sprout. P3 (Pothos) is present with the rabbit anchor.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains dark, damp soil with no visible plant matter. Status: Dormant/Lost.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Combined: Black pot contains a single, etiolated, chlorotic sprout. Status: Critical/Failing.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos leaf remains stable but shows no signs of new growth. Status: Stable.

biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot (P2/P4). A small electronic component (IC/header) appears on the desk surface in T-4 and T-3, then disappears.

temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Electronic component removed from desk surface.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Significant lighting drop (underexposure), making observation difficult.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Lighting restored; the sprout in the black pot appears slightly more desiccated.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline. The lack of foliage in P1 and the singular, weak sprout in the black pot suggest poor light levels or root-zone failure. The Pothos (P3) is the only specimen maintaining structural integrity.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot is a non-standard intervention. The electronic component on the desk is an external, non-botanical anomaly.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is failing to thrive. The registry is largely aspirational, as most specimens have either perished or failed to germinate. The Pothos leaf is the only healthy indicator. The soil appears consistently wet, which, combined with the lack of growth, may indicate overwatering or lack of light-driven transpiration.
